title: cerastium semidecandrum subsp. macilentum
intro: Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. macilentum, also known as the weak-stemmed mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) that is commonly found in dry, grassy areas and rocky places. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cerastium semidecandrum subsp. macilentum include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally to reap its benefits.
